title: Order tasks by priority list automatically
excerpt:
Thanks in advance for your help, 
I want to automatically order a list of tasks in a project, when I select high priority it jumps to the top of the list. 
Is this possible?

posts.author: designjd (Jon Docksey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks in advance for your help,
I want to automatically order a list of tasks in a project, when I select high priority it jumps to the top of the list.
Is this possible?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@designjd,

One way to do this is to sort by the priority column and save this sort as a view. Here is a explanation of how sorting works in monday. Take a look at the section “Save your sort to a new view”:
How to sort columns and items – Support (monday.com)

When sorting by a status column, items will be sorted in the order that the values occur when you edit the labels. See this article for more info on this:
The Status Column – Support (monday.com)
